Wait, but in Java, the function is called canIWin, which is a boolean function.

So, the code outline is:

public boolean canIWin(int max, int desiredTotal) {

    if (desiredTotal == 0) return true;

    int sum = max*(max+1)/2;

    if (sum < desiredTotal) return false;

    // proceed with recursive approach

    // create a memoization map

    Map<Integer, Map<Integer, Boolean>> memo = new HashMap<>();

    return helper(0, 0, max, desiredTotal, memo);

}

private boolean helper(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desiredTotal, Map<Integer, Map<Integer, Boolean>> memo) {

    if (currentSum >= desiredTotal) return true;

    Map<Integer, Boolean> sumMap = memo.get(mask);

    if (sumMap != null) {

        Boolean result = sumMap.get(currentSum);

        if (result != null) return result;

    }

    for (int i=1; i<=max; i++) {

        if ((mask & (1 << (i-1))) == 0) {

            int newSum = currentSum + i;

            int newMask = mask | (1 << (i-1));

            if (newSum >= desiredTotal) {

                // current player wins

                if (!sumMap.containsKey(currentSum)) sumMap = new HashMap<>();

                sumMap.put(currentSum, true);

                memo.put(mask, sumMap);

                return true;

            }

            boolean canWin = helper(newSum, newMask, max, desiredTotal, memo);

            if (!canWin) {

                if (!sumMap.containsKey(currentSum)) sumMap = new HashMap<>();

                sumMap.put(currentSum, true);

                memo.put(mask, sumMap);

                return true;

            }

        }

    }

    // if none of the moves leads to a win

    if (!sumMap.containsKey(currentSum)) sumMap = new HashMap<>();

    sumMap.put(currentSum, false);

public boolean canIWin(int maxChoosableInteger, int desiredTotal) {

    if (desiredTotal == 0) return true;

    int sum = maxChoosableInteger * (maxChoosableInteger + 1) / 2;

    return canWin(0, 0, maxChoosableInteger, desiredTotal, new HashMap<>());

}

private boolean canWin(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desiredTotal, Map<Integer, Boolean> memo) {

    if (currentSum >= desiredTotal) return true;

    int key = mask; // perhaps combine currentSum and mask into a single key

    if (memo.containsKey(key)) {

        return memo.get(key);

    }

    for (int i=1; i<=max; i++) {

        if ((mask & (1 << (i-1))) == 0) {

            int newSum = currentSum + i;

            int newMask = mask | (1 << (i-1));

            if (newSum >= desiredTotal) {

                memo.put(key, true);

                return true;

            }

            if (!canWin(newSum, newMask, max, desiredTotal, memo)) {

                memo.put(key, true);

                return true;

            }

        }

    }

    memo.put(key, false);

    return false;

}

public boolean canIWin(int max, int desiredTotal) {

    if (desiredTotal == 0) return true;

    int sum = max*(max+1)/2;

    if (sum < desiredTotal) return false;

    // proceed with recursive approach

    // create a memoization map

    Map<Integer, Map<Integer, Boolean>> memo = new HashMap<>();

    return helper(0, 0, max, desiredTotal, memo);

}

private boolean helper(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desiredTotal, Map<Integer, Map<Integer, Boolean>> memo) {

    if (currentSum >= desiredTotal) return true;

    Map<Integer, Boolean> sumMap = memo.get(mask);

    if (sumMap != null) {

        Boolean result = sumMap.get(currentSum);

        if (result != null) return result;

    }

    for (int i=1; i<=max; i++) {

        if ((mask & (1 << (i-1))) == 0) {

            int newSum = currentSum + i;

            int newMask = mask | (1 << (i-1));

            if (newSum >= desiredTotal) {

                // current player wins

                if (!sumMap.containsKey(currentSum)) sumMap = new HashMap<>();

                sumMap.put(currentSum, true);

                memo.put(mask, sumMap);

                return true;

            }

            boolean canWin = helper(newSum, newMask, max, desiredTotal, memo);

            if (!canWin) {

                if (!sumMap.containsKey(currentSum)) sumMap = new HashMap<>();

                sumMap.put(currentSum, true);

                memo.put(mask, sumMap);

                return true;

            }

        }

    }

    // if none of the moves leads to a win

    if (!sumMap.containsKey(currentSum)) sumMap = new HashMap<>();

    sumMap.put(currentSum, false);

    memo.put(mask, sumMap);

    return false;

}

public boolean canIWin(int maxChoosableInteger, int desiredTotal) {

    if (desiredTotal == 0) return true;

    int sum = maxChoosableInteger * (maxChoosableInteger + 1) / 2;

    if (sum < desiredTotal) return false;

    return canWin(0, 0, maxChoosableInteger, desiredTotal, new HashMap<>());

}

private boolean canWin(int currentSum, int mask, int max, int desiredTotal, Map<Integer, Boolean> memo) {

    if (currentSum >= desiredTotal) return true;

    int key = mask; // perhaps combine currentSum and mask into a single key

    if (memo.containsKey(key)) {

        return memo.get(key);

    }

    for (int i=1; i<=max; i++) {

        if ((mask & (1 << (i-1))) == 0) {

            int newSum = currentSum + i;

            int newMask = mask | (1 << (i-1));

            if (newSum >= desiredTotal) {

                memo.put(key, true);

                return true;

            }

            if (!canWin(newSum, newMask, max, desiredTotal, memo)) {

                memo.put(key, true);

                return true;

            }

        }

    }

    memo.put(key, false);

    return false;

}
